paper_overview.citation: Samui, S., Mallik, A., Kumar, D., Rao, S., Dhanya, C. T., & Gosain, A. K. (2025). Identifying the pollution sources of River Yamuna through field observations and participatory approach. J. Earth Syst. Sci., 134(41). https://doi.org/10.1007/s12040-024-02498-7
paper_overview.study_region: National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi, India, specifically the Barapullah drainage basin

data_sources.location: Delhi Jal Board (DJB) water quality laboratory, Office of the Chief Water Analyst III, Okhla Tank, Mathura Road, New Delhi-110020
data_sources.description: Water quality parameters (BOD) of samples from 43 monitoring locations

data_sources.location: Shuttle Radar Topography Mission Digital Elevation Model (SRTM DEM)
data_sources.description: 30-m resolution DEM for watershed and stream network delineation

data_sources.location: Department of Irrigation & Flood Control (I&FC), Govt. of Delhi

data_sources.description: Spatial map of Jhugri Jhogri Colonies (JJCs, informal settlements/slums)
Qualitative interviews regarding pollution sources in major pollution hotspots

temporal_coverage.sampling_dates: non-monsoon period from January to May 2022, mostly collected in the peak hours in the morning (9 am-12 pm)
temporal_coverage.granularity: One-time sampling for 43 locations; twice for 13 hotspot locations

extraction_notes: Only Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) values were reported in the results, although Total Organic Carbon (TOC) and Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD) were mentioned as crucial indicators. The source_quote for sites is a general statement about their selection, as specific quotes about the site description itself were not consistently available for all 43 sites, only for pollution sources in hotspots.
